clusters.title: Suspect in Detroit-area synagogue attack is killed by security, officials say
clusters.neutral_headline: Suspect Killed After Ramming Vehicle Into Synagogue

items.title: Suspect in Detroit-area synagogue attack is killed by security, officials say
items.standfirst: A suspect in an attack on a synagogue in the Detroit area is dead after ramming a vehicle into the building and being confronted by synagogue security, Oakland County Sheriff Michael Bouchard said.
items.content: A suspect in an attack on a synagogue in the Detroit area is dead after ramming a vehicle into the building and being confronted by synagogue security, Oakland County Sheriff Michael Bouchard said.

items.title: Suspect dead after ramming vehicle into synagogue in Michigan
items.standfirst: The FBI said the incident is being investigated as a "targeted act of violence against the Jewish community".
items.content: The FBI said the incident is being investigated as a "targeted act of violence against the Jewish community".

items.title: Suspect in synagogue attack lost family in recent airstrike in Lebanon, source says
items.standfirst: The attacker rammed a vehicle into Temple Israel in West Bloomfield and was confronted and killed by security, Oakland County Sheriff Michael Bouchard said.
items.content: The attacker rammed a vehicle into Temple Israel in West Bloomfield and was confronted and killed by security, Oakland County Sheriff Michael Bouchard said.

items.title: Suspect dead after ramming vehicle into Michigan synagogue, officials say
items.standfirst: FBI treating incident as targeted act against Jewish community as temple says all students and staff safeSign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inboxA man who rammed his vehicle into a Michigan synagogue and drove through a hallway on Thursday died during the incident, officials said.There were no other serious casualties at the Temple Israel in West Bloomfield township, a suburb in Oakland county, and the FBI said it was treating the matter as a “targeted act of violence against the Jewish community”. It was not immediately clear how the driver died, but officials said security staff engaged the suspect and at least one fired shots. Continue reading...
items.content: FBI treating incident as targeted act against Jewish community as temple says all students and staff safeSign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inboxA man who rammed his vehicle into a Michigan synagogue and drove through a hallway on Thursday died during the incident, officials said.There were no other serious casualties at the Temple Israel in West Bloomfield township, a suburb in Oakland county, and the FBI said it was treating the matter as a “targeted act of violence against the Jewish community”. It was not immediately clear how the driver died, but officials said security staff engaged the suspect and at least one fired shots. Continue reading...

items.title: What happened at the Michigan synagogue? #world
items.standfirst: Attack at Temple Israel in West Bloomfield A vehicle was driven into the entrance of Temple Israel, a large Reform synagogue in West Bloomfield Township, Michigan, and gunfire followed. Security officers at the building engaged the attacker; law enforcement later said the suspect was killed.…
items.content: Attack at Temple Israel in West Bloomfield A vehicle was driven into the entrance of Temple Israel, a large Reform synagogue in West Bloomfield Township, Michigan, and gunfire followed. items.title: Michigan synagogue ramming an ‘act of targeted violence’: FBI
items.standfirst: The FBI says it is treating a vehicle ramming at a Michigan synagogue as an attack on the Jewish community.
items.content: The FBI says it is treating a vehicle ramming at a Michigan synagogue as an attack on the Jewish community.

items.title: Man fatally shot after ramming pickup truck into Michigan synagogue
items.standfirst: Police shot and killed a man on Thursday after he drove a vehicle into a synagogue in West Bloomfield, a suburb of Detroit, Michigan, where a large-scale law enforcement operation is now under way. The city's county sheriff confirmed the fatality and reported that a security guard had also been injured.
items.content: Police shot and killed a man on Thursday after he drove a vehicle into a synagogue in West Bloomfield, a suburb of Detroit, Michigan, where a large-scale law enforcement operation is now under way. The city's county sheriff confirmed the fatality and reported that a security guard had also been injured.

items.title: FBI says its taking over the Michigan synagogue attack investigation
items.standfirst: Security officers at Temple Israel "engaged with the suspect" after a vehicle rammed into the building, according to Oakland County Sheriff Mike Bouchard.
items.content: Security officers at Temple Israel "engaged with the suspect" after a vehicle rammed into the building, according to Oakland County Sheriff Mike Bouchard.(Image credit: Emily Elconin)

clusters.title: Brave ROTC students credited with stopping deadly shooting at Old Dominion
clusters.neutral_headline: ROTC Students Stop Deadly Shooting At Old Dominion

items.title: Brave ROTC students credited with stopping deadly shooting at Old Dominion
items.standfirst: Officials praised the "brave" actions of ROTC students who confronted a gunman Thursday after he opened fire in a classroom​ on the campus of Old Dominion University, killing one person and injuring two others.
items.content: Officials praised the "brave" actions of ROTC students who confronted a gunman Thursday after he opened fire in a classroom​ on the campus of Old Dominion University, killing one person and injuring two others.

items.title: Fatal shooting at Virginia university investigated as act of terrorism, FBI says
items.standfirst: Suspect who was convicted in 2016 for supporting Islamic State is dead after attack kills one and leaves two injured Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inboxThe suspect who killed one person and injured two others at Old Dominion University on Thursday was identified by authorities as Mohamed Jalloh, a former member of the army national guard who pleaded guilty in 2016 to attempting to provide material support to the Islamic State.Dominique Evans, special agent in charge of the FBI’s Norfolk field office, told reporters the suspect had attempted to commit an “act of terrorism” and shouted “Allahu Akbar” before opening fire. He was subdued and killed by members of the university’s ROTC program in a university classroom, she said, praising them for demonstrating “extreme bravery and courage” and preventing further loss of life. (ROTC is a college-based program that allows students to train to become a US military officer while also earning a college degree.) Continue reading...
items.content: Suspect who was convicted in 2016 for supporting Islamic State is dead after attack kills one and leaves two injured Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inboxThe suspect who killed one person and injured two others at Old Dominion University on Thursday was identified by authorities as Mohamed Jalloh, a former member of the army national guard who pleaded guilty in 2016 to attempting to provide material support to the Islamic State.Dominique Evans, special agent in charge of the FBI’s Norfolk field office, told reporters the suspect had attempted to commit an “act of terrorism” and shouted “Allahu Akbar” before opening fire. He was subdued and killed by members of the university’s ROTC program in a university classroom, she said, praising them for demonstrating “extreme bravery and courage” and preventing further loss of life. (ROTC is a college-based program that allows students to train to become a US military officer while also earning a college degree.) Continue reading...

items.title: Former FBI special agent says anytime the U.S. is at war, threat environment is heightened
items.standfirst: An armed suspect with explosives rammed a truck into a Michigan synagogue on Thursday before a security guard shot and killed the suspect. Meanwhile, a deadly shooting at Virginia's Old Dominion University, also on Thursday, is being investigated as an act of terrorism. Former FBI special agent Jeff Harp joins CBS News to discuss both attacks.
items.content: An armed suspect with explosives rammed a truck into a Michigan synagogue on Thursday before a security guard shot and killed the suspect. Meanwhile, a deadly shooting at Virginia's Old Dominion University, also on Thursday, is being investigated as an act of terrorism. Former FBI special agent Jeff Harp joins CBS News to discuss both attacks.

items.title: Who was the Old Dominion shooter? #world

The attacker was shot dead at the scene after the…

items.title: FBI opens terrorism investigation after deadly shooting at Virginia university
items.standfirst: The FBI opened a terrorism investigation after a gunman opened fire Thursday at Old Dominion University in Norfolk, Virginia, killing one person and wounding two US Army personnel, officials said. The suspect, previously convicted of aiding terrorists, was subdued by ROTC students after entering a classroom and asking if it was ROTC class, officials.
items.content: The FBI opened a terrorism investigation after a gunman opened fire Thursday at Old Dominion University in Norfolk, Virginia, killing one person and wounding two US Army personnel, officials said. The suspect, previously convicted of aiding terrorists, was subdued by ROTC students after entering a classroom and asking if it was ROTC class, officials.

ai_summary: Suspect killed one person and injured two others at Old Dominion
ROTC students subdued and killed the suspect in a classroom
Incident being investigated as an act of terrorism
Suspect had previously pleaded guilty to supporting Islamic State
ROTC students demonstrated extreme bravery and courage

items.title: Israeli military drops charges against soldiers accused of abusing Palestinian detainee
items.standfirst: The Israeli military's top lawyer cites "exceptional circumstances" for dropping the case involving a Palestinian man held at Sde Teiman military prison.
items.content: The Israeli military's top lawyer cites "exceptional circumstances" for dropping the case involving a Palestinian man held at Sde Teiman military prison.

items.title: Israeli military drops charges against soldiers accused of Gaza detainee abuse
items.standfirst: Five soldiers were indicted over alleged violent abuse and rape of Palestinian man at detention centre in 2024Israel’s top military lawyer has dropped all charges against five soldiers accused of the violent abuse and rape of a Palestinian detainee from Gaza.The military advocate general, Itay Offir, said prosecutors lacked key evidence after the victim was sent back to Gaza, and that the conduct of senior officials had affected the chance of holding a fair trial. Continue reading...
items.content: Five soldiers were indicted over alleged violent abuse and rape of Palestinian man at detention centre in 2024Israel’s top military lawyer has dropped all charges against five soldiers accused of the violent abuse and rape of a Palestinian detainee from Gaza.The military advocate general, Itay Offir, said prosecutors lacked key evidence after the victim was sent back to Gaza, and that the conduct of senior officials had affected the chance of holding a fair trial. Continue reading...

ai_summary: Israeli military dropped charges against five soldiers accused of abuse
Charges were dropped due to exceptional circumstances
Victim was sent back to Gaza and key evidence was lacking
Military advocate general cited lack of evidence for dropping charges
Conduct of senior officials affected the chance of a fair trial

items.title: Iran to allow India-flagged tankers to pass through Strait of Hormuz (reports)
items.standfirst: Iran will reportedly allow Indian-flagged tankers to transit the Strait of Hormuz, a conduit for 40% of the South Asian nation's crude imports, an Indian government source told Reuters on Thursday, but an Iranian source outside the country denied any such ​deal was reached. FRANCE 24's Mark Owen speaks with Giacome Luciani, Professor at the Paris School of International Affairs.
items.content: Iran will reportedly allow Indian-flagged tankers to transit the Strait of Hormuz, a conduit for 40% of the South Asian nation's crude imports, an Indian government source told Reuters on Thursday, but an Iranian source outside the country denied any such ​deal was reached. FRANCE 24's Mark Owen speaks with Giacome Luciani, Professor at the Paris School of International Affairs.

Indian government source confirmed the agreement
Iranian source outside the country denied any such deal

clusters.title: Israel strikes Beirut and orders south Lebanon evacuation as conflict mounts
clusters.neutral_headline: Israel Strikes Beirut And Orders South Lebanon Evacuation

items.title: Israel strikes Beirut and orders south Lebanon evacuation as conflict mounts
items.standfirst: Latest order comes after Hezbollah and Iran launch attack on more than 50 targets including Israeli military basesMiddle East live – latest updatesIsrael issued a sweeping new displacement order for southern Lebanon, instructing residents up to 25 miles away from their border to head north, and striking the centre of Beirut in a sharp escalation of its fight with Hezbollah.A spokesperson for the Israeli military on Thursday ordered all residents to head north of the Zahrani River “for their safety”, before it began a bombing campaign against what it said were Hezbollah targets. Continue reading...
items.content: Latest order comes after Hezbollah and Iran launch attack on more than 50 targets including Israeli military basesMiddle East live – latest updatesIsrael issued a sweeping new displacement order for southern Lebanon, instructing residents up to 25 miles away from their border to head north, and striking the centre of Beirut in a sharp escalation of its fight with Hezbollah.A spokesperson for the Israeli military on Thursday ordered all residents to head north of the Zahrani River “for their safety”, before it began a bombing campaign against what it said were Hezbollah targets. Continue reading...

items.title: Israel strikes central Beirut after threatening to expand Lebanon operations
items.standfirst: Israel renewed its strikes on Beirut on Thursday, as it threatened to expand operations and seize territory in Lebanon if Hezbollah did not stop its attacks. The Israeli military issued a call to evacuate ahead of the Beirut attack, after having also widened its evacuation warning for residents in southern Lebanon.
items.content: Israel renewed its strikes on Beirut on Thursday, as it threatened to expand operations and seize territory in Lebanon if Hezbollah did not stop its attacks. The Israeli military issued a call to evacuate ahead of the Beirut attack, after having also widened its evacuation warning for residents in southern Lebanon.

ai_summary: Israel issued a displacement order for southern Lebanon
Residents were instructed to head north for their safety
Israeli military struck the center of Beirut
Hezbollah and Iran launched an attack on Israeli targets
Conflict is escalating in the region
